target.name: Monarch Quantum

methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Growth → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.2x) to $115M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Quantum Computing (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 5 same-sector peers exist in Quantum Computing, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
Monarch Quantum is a Growth-stage quantum computing company founded by Stanford-trained physicist Dr. Timothy Day, who previously co-founded and exited Daylight Solutions to Leonardo DRS in 2017. The company has raised $115M+ in combined capital and contracts. The quantum computing sector includes 3 public peers (IONQ, RGTI, QBTS) and 5 private competitors (Quantinuum, IonQ, D-Wave Quantum, Rigetti Computing, Infleqtion).

Stage-progression methodology is the applicable valuation technique here. Using the $115M total raised as a base valuation proxy and applying a 1.2x step-up multiplier typical for Growth-stage advancement yields $138M. Peer-median comparison is not applicable due to insufficient comparable datapoints—while 5 quantum computing peers exist in the broader sector, none satisfy same-stage, adjacent-stage, or similar valuation-tier filters necessary for meaningful triangulation (minimum 3 required).

The analysis supports a valuation range of $117M to $159M, with a median of $138M. This relatively narrow range reflects reliance on a single applicable method. Key uncertainties include the absence of recent financing data to validate the $115M proxy, lack of operational metrics (revenue, customer concentration, R&D efficiency), and the nascent state of quantum computing commercialization, which limits peer comparability. A substantive revision would require either recent Series financing data or sufficient private-market comps at equivalent stage and scale.
